tasks.description: Create packages/kumo-mcp package scaffold — Worker entrypoint, wrangler.jsonc with Durable Object binding, package.json with dependencies (agents, @modelcontextprotocol/sdk, @mcp-ui/server, @cloudflare/vite-plugin, zod), tsconfig.json, vite.config.ts
steps: packages/kumo-mcp/ directory exists with worker/index.ts, wrangler.jsonc, package.json, tsconfig.json, vite.config.ts
wrangler.jsonc has durable_objects binding for McpAgent subclass and new_sqlite_classes migration
package.json has agents, @modelcontextprotocol/sdk, @mcp-ui/server, @cloudflare/vite-plugin, zod as dependencies
pnpm install succeeds from monorepo root
TypeScript compiles without errors (pnpm --filter kumo-mcp typecheck or tsc --noEmit)

tasks.description: McpAgent subclass (KumoPlaygroundMCP) with init() method, Worker entrypoint routing /mcp to the DO, static serve()
steps: worker/mcp/index.ts exports KumoPlaygroundMCP extending McpAgent
KumoPlaygroundMCP has server = new McpServer({...}) and async init() stub
worker/index.ts routes /mcp to KumoPlaygroundMCP.serve('/mcp', {binding: '...'})
wrangler dev starts without errors (pnpm --filter kumo-mcp dev)
MCP endpoint responds to POST /mcp with valid MCP protocol response (e.g. initialize)

tasks.description: Register create_worker tool that returns a UI resource (externalUrl) with uiMetadata containing initial-render-data (workerName, toolId) and preferred-frame-size
steps: worker/mcp/tools.ts exports initializeTools(agent) that registers create_worker tool
Tool accepts { workerName: z.string() } input schema
Tool returns content array with createUIResource result of type externalUrl
uiMetadata includes initial-render-data: { workerName, toolId: 'create-worker-{workerName}' }
uiMetadata includes preferred-frame-size: ['100%', '280px']
Calling tools/call with name=create_worker, arguments={workerName:'hello-world'} via MCP client returns the UI resource

tasks.description: Register execute_create_worker tool with mock 800ms execution returning structuredContent { success, workerName, createdAt }
steps: execute_create_worker tool registered in initializeTools
Tool accepts { workerName: z.string() } input
Tool returns content with text 'Worker "{name}" created successfully.'
Tool returns structuredContent: { success: true, workerName, createdAt: ISO8601 }
Execution takes ~800ms (mock delay)
Calling tools/call with name=execute_create_worker via MCP client returns structured result

tasks.description: Iframe-side postMessage utilities: sendMcpMessage (typed, messageId-correlated, Zod-validated responses) and waitForRenderData (lifecycle: ready signal → receive render data → Zod parse)
steps: app/utils/mcp.ts (or packages/kumo-mcp/app/utils/mcp.ts) exports sendMcpMessage function
sendMcpMessage posts { type, messageId, payload } to window.parent and returns Promise
sendMcpMessage resolves when receiving ui-message-response with matching messageId
sendMcpMessage validates response with optional Zod schema before resolving
waitForRenderData exported — posts ui-lifecycle-iframe-ready, listens for ui-lifecycle-iframe-render-data
waitForRenderData validates renderData with Zod schema
All postMessage types are TypeScript-typed (no any)

tasks.description: useMcpUiInit hook: signals iframe readiness and reports initial dimensions to host via postMessage
steps: useMcpUiInit(rootRef) exported from iframe-side utils
On mount, posts { type: 'ui-lifecycle-iframe-ready' } to window.parent
On mount, reads rootRef dimensions and posts { type: 'ui-size-change', payload: { height, width } }
Hook cleans up properly on unmount

tasks.description: Host-side postMessage handler: createIframeMessageHandler that handles ui-lifecycle-iframe-ready (sends render data), tool messages (delegates to callback), ui-size-change (resize callback)
steps: src/lib/mcp-host.ts (in kumo-docs-astro) exports createIframeMessageHandler(config)
Handler responds to ui-lifecycle-iframe-ready by posting ui-lifecycle-iframe-render-data with config.renderData
Handler receives tool messages and calls config.onToolCall(toolName, params) callback
Handler sends ui-message-response with result from onToolCall back to iframe (correlated by messageId)
Handler calls config.onResize(height, width) on ui-size-change
Handler filters messages by iframe source (event.source check)
All types are TypeScript-typed

tasks.description: Confirmation iframe React app scaffold — Vite + React Router, route at /ui/create-worker-confirm, clientLoader that calls waitForRenderData, HydrateFallback with loader
steps: packages/kumo-mcp/app/ directory has routes/ui/create-worker-confirm.tsx
Route exports clientLoader that calls waitForRenderData with Zod schema for { workerName, toolId }
Route exports HydrateFallback showing a loading state
Route exports default component that useLoaderData to access workerName and toolId
Vite config builds the iframe app (React + React Router)
App loads in browser at /ui/create-worker-confirm (via wrangler dev or Vite dev)

tasks.description: Streaming confirmation card inside iframe — fetches /api/chat with TOOL_CONFIRMATION_PROMPT, streams JSONL via readSSEStream + createJsonlParser + applyPatch, renders with UITreeRenderer
steps: streamConfirmationCard function fetches /api/chat with skipSystemPrompt:true + systemPromptOverride
TOOL_CONFIRMATION_PROMPT included (generates compact approval card with tool_approve/tool_cancel actions)
Response parsed via readSSEStream → createJsonlParser → applyPatch pipeline
UITree state updates progressively as patches arrive (streaming UX)
UITreeRenderer renders the tree with streaming=true during load
On stream complete, status transitions from streaming → pending

tasks.description: Iframe action handling — Approve calls sendMcpMessage to host for execute_create_worker, Cancel notifies host. Status-based overlays (applying/completed/cancelled)
steps: Approve button action (tool_approve) sets status to applying and calls sendMcpMessage('tool', {toolName:'execute_create_worker', params:{workerName}}, {schema})
On success (result.success===true), status transitions to completed with CheckIcon overlay
Cancel button action (tool_cancel) sets status to cancelled and sends cancel message to host
Cancelled state shows greyed-out card (opacity + pointer-events-none)
Applying state shows semi-transparent overlay with Loader spinner
Only pending status allows button interaction

tasks.description: McpToolIframe React component — renders <iframe> in chat sidebar, manages postMessage lifecycle, delegates tool actions to parent, applies status-based styling
steps: McpToolIframe component in kumo-docs-astro accepts src, renderData, status, onToolAction, onStatusChange props
Renders <iframe src={src} sandbox='allow-scripts allow-same-origin'>
Sets up createIframeMessageHandler on mount, cleans up on unmount
Handles iframe ready → sends render data
Handles tool messages → calls onToolAction prop
Handles size changes → adjusts iframe height
Applies status-based CSS: streaming shows loader, cancelled shows opacity, applying shows overlay

tasks.description: MCP proxy endpoint at /api/mcp-proxy — thin POST handler that forwards tool calls to the kumo-mcp Worker (via service binding or fetch) and returns MCP tool result
steps: src/pages/api/mcp-proxy.ts in kumo-docs-astro exports POST handler
Accepts { toolName: string, params: Record<string, unknown> } body
Forwards as MCP tools/call JSON-RPC to kumo-mcp server
Returns tool result (content + structuredContent) to caller
Rate limiting applied (reuse CHAT_RATE_LIMIT or separate)
Error cases return appropriate HTTP status + error body

tasks.description: Update ToolChatMessage type to carry iframeUrl + renderData instead of inline UITree. Update chat message rendering to use McpToolIframe for tool messages.
steps: ToolChatMessage interface has iframeUrl: string and renderData?: Record<string, unknown> instead of tree: UITree
Chat sidebar renders McpToolIframe for role==='tool' messages
McpToolIframe gets src=msg.iframeUrl, renderData=msg.renderData, status=msg.status
Old inline UITreeRenderer rendering for tool messages removed

tasks.description: Wire submit handler intercept — when message matches CREATE_WORKER_PATTERN, call MCP proxy to get UI resource, extract iframeUrl + renderData from response, create ToolChatMessage with iframe
steps: handleSubmit intercept calls /api/mcp-proxy with toolName=create_worker, params={workerName}
Response parsed to extract iframeUrl from UI resource content
renderData extracted from uiMetadata['initial-render-data']
ToolChatMessage created with iframeUrl, renderData, status='streaming'
Message added to chat — McpToolIframe renders and iframe loads

tasks.description: Wire tool action flow — McpToolIframe's onToolAction delegates to MCP proxy for execute_create_worker. On success, fire follow-up prompt to A/B panels. On cancel, update status.
steps: handleToolAction receives tool action from McpToolIframe
For tool_approve: calls /api/mcp-proxy with toolName=execute_create_worker
MCP proxy returns structuredContent with success:true
On success: status → completed, then follow-up prompt fired to handleSubmit for A/B panels
For tool_cancel: status → cancelled, no MCP call, no panel generation
Follow-up prompt avoids CREATE_WORKER_PATTERN to prevent re-intercept
All 6 existing pills continue to work (no regression)

tasks.description: Extract chat types (ChatMessage, ToolChatMessage, TextChatMessage, ToolMessageStatus) from _PlaygroundPage.tsx into src/lib/chat-types.ts
steps: src/lib/chat-types.ts exists in kumo-docs-astro with all chat message types exported
_PlaygroundPage.tsx imports from chat-types.ts instead of defining inline
No duplicate type definitions
TypeScript compiles without errors

tasks.description: Extract tool middleware (matchCreateWorkerMessage, handleToolAction, updateToolMessageStatus, status FSM) into src/lib/tool-middleware.ts
steps: src/lib/tool-middleware.ts exports matchCreateWorkerMessage, updateToolMessageStatus, CREATE_WORKER_PATTERN
handleToolAction logic extracted (may stay partially in PlaygroundPage for React state access)
_PlaygroundPage.tsx imports from tool-middleware.ts
No behavioral change — same flow, just extracted

tasks.description: Extract tool prompts (TOOL_CONFIRMATION_PROMPT, BASELINE_PROMPT, follow-up prompt templates) into src/lib/tool-prompts.ts
steps: src/lib/tool-prompts.ts exports TOOL_CONFIRMATION_PROMPT and follow-up prompt builder
BASELINE_PROMPT stays in PlaygroundPage or also extracted (it's used by Panel B, not tool-specific)
_PlaygroundPage.tsx imports prompts instead of defining inline
No behavioral change

tasks.description: Tool registry pattern — ToolDefinition interface + TOOL_REGISTRY map. handleSubmit uses registry to match messages and route to MCP tools. Pill list derived from registry.
steps: src/lib/tool-registry.ts exports ToolDefinition interface with match, mcpToolName, mcpExecuteToolName, followUpPrompt, pill fields
TOOL_REGISTRY exported as ReadonlyMap with create_worker entry
handleSubmit iterates TOOL_REGISTRY to find matching tool instead of hardcoded CREATE_WORKER_PATTERN check
PRESET_PROMPTS includes pills from TOOL_REGISTRY entries
Adding a hypothetical second tool entry to TOOL_REGISTRY would work without modifying _PlaygroundPage.tsx

context.branchWarning: CRITICAL: All work MUST be on branch geoquant/streaming-ui. Do NOT create new branches or use main.
patterns: Streaming pipeline: readSSEStream → createJsonlParser → applyPatch → UITreeRenderer
nonGoals: Real Cloudflare API calls (create_worker stays mocked)
Changing A/B panel architecture (panels still stream from /api/chat)
